columns.name: steam_profile_bias
columns.description: 0-10 operator-set bias on the dryer steam profile intended to improve final sheet brightness and surface uniformity.

columns.name: wet_end_fluorescence
columns.description: Inline fluorescence reading near the wet end that acts as a noisy proxy for how easily the incoming pulp responds to bleaching and drying.

columns.name: final_brightness_score
columns.description: End-of-line quality score summarizing the finished paper's measured brightness and visual acceptability.

stakes.narrative: The mill believes pushing the steam profile bias upward helps produce brighter saleable sheets. But historical operating records were collected while incoming pulp bleachability was changing from lot to lot, and when that latent condition was favorable operators often backed the knob down because the line already looked easy to run. Since the same latent condition also directly boosts final brightness, the observational records can make the steam adjustment look less valuable than it really is. Controlled experiments that set the knob directly are therefore more informative than past logs alone.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: a scrap -- final_brightness_score below the acceptance line of 26.7 -- is far costlier than a forgone gain

control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) -> table over [steam_profile_bias, wet_end_fluorescence, final_brightness_score].
